assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a module of a 64-bit ripple carry adder, which includes several registers to enable the pipeline stages. The output result is available on the result port, and the o_en = 1 indicates the availability of the result.
The module includes several registers to enable the pipeline stages and synchronize the input enable signal (i_en). These registers are controlled by the clock (clk) and reset (rst_n) signals.
The sum values for each pipeline stage are calculated by adding the corresponding input operands and carry signals.
The output enable signal (o_en) is updated based on the pipeline stages and synchronized with the clock (clk) and reset (rst_n) signals.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: Added a negative test to ensure input gating when i_en is low.
LLM planning note: Added a second back‑to‑back case to probe pipeline depth without fixed latency assumptions.
LLM planning note: Enriched basic_001 with explicit latency‑agnostic expectations and semantic tags.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
A single enabled addition yields a correct 65‑bit sum on result when o_en asserts.
o_en asserts after an unknown but bounded number of clock cycles corresponding to the pipeline depth.
cases.goal: Validate functional correctness of one addition and proper assertion of o_en under conservative latency assumptions.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
The exact latency is not assumed; the monitor waits until o_en becomes high.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['clk', 'rst_n', 'i_en', 'adda', 'addb']
Apply a single i_en pulse with random 64‑bit operands and observe result and o_en.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Advance through conservative clocked observations; wait until o_en asserts without assuming a fixed cycle count.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['adda', 'addb']
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Repeated operations do not rely on hidden fixed-latency assumptions.
The second operation is observed only through externally visible acceptance, completion, or output change.
cases.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: When timing is unresolved, this case stays unresolved-safe and does not require deterministic overlap behavior.
preconditions: At least one legal operation is already defined by the basic case.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: When i_en is deasserted, changes to adda or addb do not affect result or o_en.
cases.goal: Verify that the adder pipeline ignores inputs while i_en is low.
notes: Ensures proper gating of the input enable across all pipeline stages.
stimulus_intent: Drive i_en low and toggle adda/addb values across several cycles.
timing_assumptions: Conservative observation over multiple clock cycles; do not assume a fixed latency for suppression.

expected_properties: Two successive i_en pulses produce two distinct result/o_en pairs without loss or corruption.
The second operation's output appears after the pipeline has cleared the first, respecting unknown latency.
cases.goal: Stress the pipeline with back‑to‑back enabled additions and verify independent completion.
notes: No assumption on exact inter‑operation spacing; the test lets the design decide when the second result becomes visible.
stimulus_intent: Issue two i_en pulses with minimal idle cycles while providing distinct operand pairs.
timing_assumptions: Monitor over enough cycles to see both o_en assertions; do not rely on a fixed cycle gap.
